steps.title: Test the universal claim
steps.content: Alvarez and colleagues argue that mapping time onto a front-back axis is not universal. To support that claim, the finding should show Yupno speakers using temporal gestures that are not anchored to a fixed front-back orientation like the one described in research on Spanish speakers.

steps.title: Use terrain-based gestures
steps.content: Future-directed gestures that change depending on whether a speaker faces uphill or downhill would show that orientation to the landscape, not a universal front-back axis, is guiding the gesture. A concerns hand choice, C concerns where gestures occur, and D concerns grammar rather than spatial gesture. The answer is B.
